title: Shalla Nelprin

<!--DO NOT EDIT THIS QUOTE. It is exactly as it appears in the original source. If you feel the need to correct it, try reading it again out loud, and pay special attention to the context.-->
{{Quote|I don't think anyone could kill a Wookiee hand to hand, sir. But she comes closer than anyone I'be seen.|[[Radaf Netbers]], after having his nose broken by Nelprin|<ref name="IronFist" />}}<!--DO NOT EDIT THIS QUOTE.-->

'''Shalla Nelprin''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] from [[Wraith Squadron]] who was selected for her proficiency in hand-to-hand combat, and for her knowledge of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] doctrine and tactics.

==Biography==
The [[Nelprin family]] was fairly well known in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]] for their proficiency in unarmed combat.<ref name="IronFist" /> Shalla's father was [[Vyn Narcassan]], a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Senate Bureau of Intelligence/Legends|intelligence]] officer who disappeared after the rise of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="WraithSquadron" /> He was hunted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] but was never found.<ref name="SoloCommand">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref> Nelprin and her sister, [[Vula Nelprin|Vula]], were both trained by Narcassan in hand-to-hand techniques, leading Vula to become a successful instructor in the art. Vula was [[Kell Tainer]]'s instructor, and could toss him around like a rag doll—Nelprin claimed she was even better, and her performance supported this.<ref name="IronFist" />

She managed to infiltrate Zsinj's headquarters, thanks to Kell's previous mention of her under an alias, during a previous meeting with Zsinj. However, due to an absent-minded boast during said meeting, she was forced to fight Captain [[Radaf Netbers]] in hand-to-hand combat. [[Garik Loran|Face]] had bragged she could beat a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]], Kell gave details, and this prompted Netbers to try and confirm it. Luckily, Nelprin's own boasts were not exaggerated, leading to Netbers having his nose broken.<ref name="IronFist" /> Shalla (posing under the alias "Qatya Nassin" for the mission) worked with several other hired pirates to infiltrate [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and steal the ''[[Razor's Kiss]]'' for Warlord [[Zsinj]]. At one point, Nelprin isolated herself from the group and faked her death using some explosives provided by other Wraiths. In the ship's hanger, Shalla located a customized [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]-equipped [[TIE/IN interceptor]] and used the vehicle as an escape craft. Parking beneath the command deck of ''Razor's Kiss'', Shalla waited until [[Mon Remonda]] starfighter forces were inbound before targeting the Dreadnought's shield generators, rendering the massive vessel vulnerable to attack.

After her participation in the [[Battle for the Razor's Kiss|Battle for the ''Razor's Kiss'']] with the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''Razor's Kiss'', Nelprin was promoted to [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and awarded half the kill of the titular ship for her participation in disabling the craft—permitting her to paint half of an ''Executor''-class silhouette on her X-wing.<ref name="IronFist" /> The TIE Interceptor captured prior to the battle would eventually be traded for the ship that would eventually be code-named ''[[Millennium Falsehood]]'' for a long-term covert project.

During the Wraiths' infiltration of [[Binring Biomedical Product]], Shalla faced Netbers a second time; the captain recognized her and she charged him to prevent discovery of her identity. Netbers nearly murdered her, except her squadron-mate [[Hohass Ekwesh]] intervened, breaking the man's neck.<ref name="SoloCommand" />

Although not present during the Wraith operations against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], Nelprin transferred to [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] with the majority of her squadron when they were recommissioned as an intelligence unit.<ref name="SoloCommand" />

Nelprin again appeared in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] as a member of Intelligence investigating [[Syal Antilles]] during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], no longer a Wraith Squadron member.<ref name="Betrayal" />
